prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: You might be wrong about the effectiveness of multitasking. Many people believe they can efficiently juggle multiple tasks at once, but research suggests that our brains are not actually wired for that. When we try to multitask, we end up rapidly switching between tasks, which can actually be less efficient and lead to more mistakes. It's often more effective to focus on one task at a time. Of course, I don't know you personally, so this may or may not apply to your specific situation. It's just a common misconception that many people have.
